name: Intake (Data Catalog and Loading Framework)

auth.notes: Local catalog; cloud auth via fsspec backends

performance.notes: Lazy loading; actual performance depends on backend (pandas, xarray, Dask, etc.)

agent_readiness.notes: Data catalog framework for reproducible data access. import intake. cat = intake.open_catalog('catalog.yaml'). ds = cat.my_dataset(). ds.read(). ds.to_dask(). Catalog YAML: sources: my_dataset: driver: csv args: urlpath: 's3://bucket/data.csv'. Drivers: CSV, Parquet, Zarr, NetCDF, HDF5, SQL, intake-xarray, intake-stac. Server: intake.server.server.IntakeServer. GUI: intake.gui. Pangeo integration. Intake-2 unified API.

gotchas: Intake 2.0 broke backward compatibility with Intake 1.x catalogs
Plugin ecosystem (intake-xarray, intake-stac) has uneven maintenance
Less adopted than alternatives like fsspec or direct xarray
Learning curve for catalog YAML syntax
best_when: Team data catalogs where reproducible, shareable data access is needed
When data sources need to be abstracted behind a catalog interface
avoid_when: Production data pipelines (use Prefect, Airflow, or DVC)
When catalog overhead isn't justified by team size
summary: Intake provides a data catalog framework for reproducible data access. intake.open_catalog('catalog.yaml'). cat.my_dataset().read(). YAML-defined data sources. Drivers for CSV, Parquet, Zarr, NetCDF, SQL. .to_dask() for lazy loading. BSD-3 by Anaconda. Best for team data catalogs in Pangeo/scientific computing workflows. Intake 2.0 broke Intake 1.x compatibility. Plugin ecosystem has uneven maintenance.
